summary: Decompiled and matched several additional ground animation routines in src/melee/gr/granime.c, including grAnime_801C752C, grAnime_801C7C1C, and grAnime_801C8578, while refining related helper calls, assertions, prototypes, and stage archive struct fields. The PR used small inline/noinline wrapper helpers to control codegen around calls that previously relied on pragma dont_inline or hand-written duplicated logic. It also replaced byte-offset archive accesses with named fields in UnkStageDat_x8_t, making later granime and stage archive work more readable and type-safe.
what_changed: Implemented previously stubbed granime functions: grAnime_801C752C, grAnime_801C7C1C, and grAnime_801C8578.

Marked target_funcs.txt with completed targets in src/melee/gr/granime.c: grAnime_801C86D4, grAnime_801C84A4, grAnime_801C8578, grAnime_801C6A54, grAnime_801C752C, grAnime_801C6C0C, grAnime_801C7228, and grAnime_801C7C1C.
Added typed archive animation-pointer fields to struct UnkStageDat_x8_t: unk4 as HSD_AnimJoint**, unk8 as HSD_MatAnimJoint**, and unkC as HSD_ShapeAnimJoint**.
Updated granime.h prototypes for grAnime_801C752C from a generic variadic declaration to HSD_JObj*, s32, s32, void*, u32, ... and for grAnime_801C8578 from void*, void* to HSD_Joint*, s32*.
Replaced manual __assert calls in touched granime paths with HSD_ASSERT or HSD_ASSERTREPORT, requiring inclusion of baselib/debug.h.
Added stdarg.h and dolphin/os.h includes to support the variadic grAnime_801C752C implementation and related code.
Refactored repeated logic in grAnime_801C7228 into inline grAnime_801C6F50_wrapped, preserving assert and aobj callback behavior while reducing duplicated code.

Introduced inner/noinline wrapper patterns around several functions such as grAnime_801C67A8, grAnime_801C683C, grAnime_801C6960, grAnime_801C69FC, grAnime_801C6A54, grAnime_801C706C, and grAnime_801C8578 to influence matching code generation.
Simplified grAnime_801C6960 by calling grAnime_801C68F4 for each robj/anim pair instead of duplicating the aobj remove/load logic inline.
Changed archive access sites in grAnime_801C8138 and grAnime_801C7C1C to use named struct fields rather than pointer arithmetic offsets.
smart_moves: Using typed fields in UnkStageDat_x8_t converted opaque offset loads at +4, +8, and +0xC into meaningful HSD_AnimJoint, HSD_MatAnimJoint, and HSD_ShapeAnimJoint arrays, reducing future decomp ambiguity.
The inline wrapper grAnime_801C6F50_wrapped isolated a repeated assert/callback/flag check sequence from grAnime_801C7228 and let the same logic be reused for the root jobj and child jobj cases.
The inner/noinline wrapper chain replaced a local #pragma dont_inline block and appears deliberately tuned for compiler output without changing the visible call graph semantics.

Keeping grAnime_801C752C's callback argument in a union-like callbackArg local and switching on AOBJ_ARG_* constants preserved the varargs dispatch pattern needed by HSD_AObjSetRate and HSD_AObjSetFlags calls.
The grAnime_801C8578 implementation made the traversal contract explicit: decrement an external counter while walking child first, then next, and return the joint when the counter reaches zero.
Updating headers alongside implementations avoided leaving decompiled functions with broad void* or underspecified variadic prototypes.
decomp_lessons: When a stage archive layout is used repeatedly through hard-coded offsets, promote those offsets into named struct fields once the pointed-to types are clear.
For matching, small static inline forwarding functions can be useful to force or prevent inlining at specific call sites, especially when old code used #pragma dont_inline.
If duplicated code is preventing a match, extracting it into an inline helper can improve both readability and codegen control, but the helper should preserve asserts and side effects exactly.
Variadic animation helpers should declare the fixed arguments precisely before ..., then use va_start on the last fixed parameter and read f64 for promoted float arguments.
Prefer project assertion macros such as HSD_ASSERT and HSD_ASSERTREPORT over raw __assert when they match existing baselib/debug conventions and expected line-number behavior.
Recursive joint traversal functions may need a pointer-to-counter parameter rather than returning an index, because mutation order affects matching and behavior.
naming_conventions: New archive fields kept provisional offset-style names unk4, unk8, and unkC while adding precise pointer types; this is consistent with decomp practice when semantics are partially known but offsets are confirmed.
Wrapper helpers used suffixes _inner, _noinline, and _wrapped to communicate codegen role rather than final game semantics.
Unmatched or still-unknown locals retained phi_rXX/temp_rXX-style names in existing code where stronger semantic names were not evidenced.
Function names remained address-based grAnime_801Cxxxx names, indicating no semantic rename was attempted in this PR.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Added static inline forwarding helpers such as grAnime_801C67A8_inner plus grAnime_801C67A8_noinline to influence call inlining and stack/code shape.
Replaced a #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop section around grAnime_801C67A8 with an explicit wrapper call grAnime_801C67A8_noinline.
Marked grAnime_801C6960 and grAnime_801C6F50_wrapped as inline, suggesting the author was matching expected inlining behavior in callers.
Removed PAD_STACK(16) from grAnime_801C6A54 after refactoring, implying the matched implementation no longer required artificial stack padding.
In grAnime_801C86D4, separate s32 stack locals plus a scoped root variable replaced an anonymous stack struct, likely to better match stack layout and register allocation.
Used typed struct field accesses instead of casts like *(HSD_ShapeAnimJoint***)((u8*)&archive->unk4->unk8[arg1] + 0xC), which can improve both readability and compiler output stability.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR context, and the human text excerpt was empty.
There was one issue comment recorded by counts, but its body was not included in the provided context, so no concrete reviewer feedback can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename UnkStageDat_x8_t fields unk4, unk8, or unkC to semantic names.
Check subsequent granime PRs for removal or retention of _inner/_noinline wrapper helpers once more functions matched.
Search for other varargs animation callback implementations using callbackArg and AOBJ_ARG_* to compare conventions.
Look for review or commit history around grAnime_801C7C1C to confirm whether arg names and flag masks var_r30/var_r29 were later documented.
